description: Extend the existing check_consistency tooling with a hygiene report surfacing: floating aims, mega-parents (more than N direct leaf children = catch-all smell), orphaned cancelled/human-dependent aims, duplicate clusters (from the duplicate finder), and done-leaf ratio per subtree (candidates for archiving/collapsing). Gives a single dashboard of where the graph needs maintenance.

reflections.context: Build a read-only graph-hygiene dashboard surfacing where the 534-aim graph needs maintenance, composing the duplicate/reparent tooling.
reflections.outcome: backend project.graphHygiene + graph_hygiene MCP tool with five sections (floating, mega-parents, stale-status, collapse candidates, duplicate clusters via union-find). Verified live; results matched known true positives (the 'UI improvements'/'ui improvements' duplicate, the 'list view' 32-child mega-parent).
reflections.effectiveness: Mirroring checkConsistency/findDuplicates kept it fast and idiomatic. Running it against the real graph immediately surfaced concrete next maintenance work (32-child list-view parent → a reparent target; 84 all-done parents → archive candidates), proving the report is actionable rather than decorative.
reflections.lesson: A maintenance/report feature is only worth shipping if its first real run points at concrete actions; validate against a known issue (here, the byte-identical duplicate) to confirm the heuristics fire. Union-find is the clean way to turn pairwise-similarity into clusters.
reflections.pattern: Same read-only-report-procedure + live-throwaway-verification approach as find_duplicate_aims and suggest_reparents; the graph-maintenance tooling now composes (hygiene report points at merge_aims / suggest_reparents / archiving).

status.comment: Implemented as read-only backend project.graphHygiene + graph_hygiene MCP tool (mirrors checkConsistency/findDuplicates). Surfaces all 5 requested sections: floating aims (no parent + uncommitted), mega-parents (≥ megaParentThreshold direct children), stale cancelled/failed/human-dependent aims, collapse candidates (parents whose active children are ALL done = the actionable end of done-leaf ratio), and duplicate clusters (all-pairs cosine ≥ threshold, grouped via union-find). Per-section caps. Verified live on the 534-aim graph: flagged 'list view' (32 children) as a mega-parent, 3 duplicate clusters incl. the known 'UI improvements'/'ui improvements', 84 collapse candidates, 56 stale, 11 floating. backend tsc + mcp tsc clean; mcp built (graph_hygiene in build/tools.js). Needs /mcp reconnect to expose. Note: done-leaf 'ratio' is reported as the all-done subset (collapse candidates) rather than every partial ratio.
